page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, alongside 236 GB of local storage. It is also Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 34.96 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.44 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 4533.06 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 29.06 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak write speed of 22.41 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though read bandwidth for large blocks remains average at 44.69 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in workloads like small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves an average 134.95 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model.

This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute power. The high memory density per vCPU makes it suitable for relational databases, caching layers like Redis (which performs at an average of 7.86 million operations per second), and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, a 480 MB L3 cache, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression at 34.96 GB/sec, while multi-core and memory write bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enabling average performance in small-scale LLM inference and Redis operations. This configuration is suitable for memory-bound workloads, database operations, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, 236 GB of local storage, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
Best suited for memory-bound applications, databases, and caching layers
